Luxury Vacation Homes on Florida's Gulf Coast

From Anna Maria Island charm to Sarasota sophistication, our rentals offer the best of the Gulf Coast.

✍️ By Seascape Vacations Team | Last Updated: 2026-03-02

Florida's Gulf Coast stretches along some of the most beautiful shoreline in the United States, and the area between Anna Maria Island and Siesta Key represents the sweet spot — world-class beaches, warm Gulf waters, a thriving arts and dining scene, and a relaxed pace of life that feels a million miles from the theme-park crowds of central Florida.

Seascape Vacations offers luxury vacation rental homes across this prime Gulf Coast corridor, covering Anna Maria Island, Bradenton, Sarasota, and the Siesta Key area. Our properties range from intimate waterfront retreats to spacious pool estates sleeping up to 16 guests, all professionally managed with the kind of personal attention you only get from a locally owned company.

What makes this stretch of the Gulf Coast special? The beaches, for starters — Siesta Key's quartz-crystal sand has been named America's #1 Beach multiple times, and Anna Maria Island's seven miles of unspoiled coastline feel like stepping back in time. Add in Sarasota's Ringling Museum, the fresh seafood scene in Cortez Village, championship golf courses, and world-class fishing, and you have a destination that delivers for every type of traveler. Book direct with Seascape Vacations and save 10–15% compared to listing platforms.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Which Gulf Coast destination is right for my vacation?

It depends on what you're looking for: Anna Maria Island for quiet, old-Florida beach charm; Bradenton for waterfront homes with docks and great value; Sarasota for arts, fine dining, and cultural experiences; Siesta Key for America's #1-rated beach. Our team can help match you with the perfect area.

How do I save money on Gulf Coast vacation rentals?

Book directly with Seascape Vacations at seascape-vacations.com to save 10–15% compared to Airbnb and VRBO. You avoid platform service fees that typically add $200–$500+ to a week-long stay. We also offer discounts for extended stays and returning guests.

What's the best time to visit Florida's Gulf Coast?

The Gulf Coast is a year-round destination. Winter (December–March) offers perfect 70–80°F weather and is ideal for snowbirds. Spring and fall bring pleasant temperatures with lower rates. Summer is warm and great for families, with afternoon thunderstorms that cool things down.

How far apart are Anna Maria Island, Bradenton, and Sarasota?

These destinations are remarkably close together. Bradenton to Anna Maria Island is 10–15 minutes. Bradenton to downtown Sarasota is about 20 minutes. Sarasota to Siesta Key is 15–20 minutes. No matter where you stay, the entire Gulf Coast corridor is easily accessible.

What makes Seascape Vacations different from other rental companies?

We're locally owned and operated by a team that lives on Florida's Gulf Coast. We personally know every property, every neighborhood, and every hidden gem. Our small portfolio means each home gets premium attention — professional cleaning, proactive maintenance, and genuine hospitality you won't get from large management companies.

Do your Gulf Coast homes have pools?

Yes! All our vacation rentals feature private heated pools, and several also include hot tubs. Unlike beachfront condos with shared pools, our homes give you a completely private pool experience along with spacious yards, outdoor dining areas, and BBQ grills.
